title: The Null-Point Awakening
content: [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'Where... am I? My head feels like it was hit by a freight train.' You open your eyes to a sky the color of a fresh bruise. You are lying in a glass-edged crater. The air is still, but you can feel a rhythmic pulsing deep in your bones. A flickering, gaseous light—a spirit fragment—hovers inches from your face. It is wounded, bleeding raw energy that smells like burnt copper and ozone. As your Earth-born logic tries to process the impossible, you feel a strange pull. The spirit isn't just near you; it's reacting to you. You are an anchor in this shifting reality.

choices.text: Reach out and stabilize the spirit's heat. (Thermal Entropy Affinity)
Anchor the spirit's erratic movement. (Vector Resonance Affinity)
Offer your own life-force to heal the flicker. (Biomorphic Flux Affinity)
Merge your mind with the spirit's sensory echo. (Cognitive Distortion Affinity)

title: The Scavenger's Shadow
content: The spirit merges into your chest with a silent, blinding flash. For a moment, you are the only thing that makes sense in this desert. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'What was that? I feel... grounded. Different.' A figure—{npc:kaelen}—is peering over the edge of the crater now, their hand drifting toward a rusted iron knife. As they take in your {player.height}, {player.bodyType} frame and the strange, steady light now radiating from your chest, they [NPC_REACT]. 'I don't know what void you leaked out of,' the figure calls down, 'but you've sucked the life right out of the air. If you want a ride out of here, you're going to have to prove you aren't just a walking curse.'

choices.text: 'Who are you? And where am I?'
'Stay back. I'm not a curse, but I can be one if you touch that knife.'
(Ignore them and climb out of the crater yourself).

title: The Lonely Outpost
content: You arrive at the Borderlands Outpost alone, your feet heavy with red dust. Without a local to vouch for you, the iron gates feel more like a cage than a sanctuary. A Syndicate guard blocks your path, their rifle humming with thermal energy. 'No strangers without a permit. Unless you've got Shards or something to trade.'

choices.text: Bribe the guard (50 Shards).
[Finesse 12] Sneak past through the ventilation ducts.

title: Interacting with {npc:active}
content: {npc:active} looks up as you approach. 'Not many folks come through here with that kind of light in their eyes,' they mutter, [NPC_REACT]. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'They seem open enough to talk. I might learn something about the local currents.'

choices.text: Listen to their stories.
Share a bit of your own history.

title: {npc:active}'s Tale
content: {npc:active} leans back, their eyes distant as they recount a fragment of their life in the Borderlands. You learn of the constant struggle for pure water and the fear of the Syndicate's 'Compliance Audits.' [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'The weight of this world is heavy on them. I can feel the resonance of their exhaustion.'

choices.text: Nod and move on.

title: Sharing the Burden
content: You speak of the world you left behind—the green trees, the blue oceans, the lack of static in the air. {npc:active} listens in stunned silence, their jaw tightening. 'A world without Burnout?' they whisper. 'You're either a saint or a very dangerous liar.'

choices.text: Let the silence hang.

title: Sifting Through Slag
content: You find a promising pile of debris—a mixture of rusted iron and shattered spirit-glass. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'There's a faint hum coming from the bottom of this pile. Something's still holding a charge.'

choices.text: Dig deep with your bare hands.
[Logic 12] Use a metal rod to probe for resonance spikes.

title: Scavenge Results
content: After an hour of digging through the rusted ribs of a fallen crawler, you emerge covered in red dust. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'Not much left here, but every shard counts.' You found {game.lastLoot}.

choices.text: Pack it away.

title: The Iron Watch Intercept
content: A high-pitched whine, like a snapping cable, cuts through the red dust. Two Syndicate Enforcers emerge from behind a rusted crawler, their brass goggles reflecting the bruise-colored sky. One of them checks a clicking device on their wrist. 'Resonance signature confirmed. You're the anomaly from the crater.' They raise their thermal-rifles. [NPC_REACT]. 'Overseer Vane doesn't like loose ends in his sector. You're coming with us to the Watch, or you're staying here as scrap.'

choices.text: Surrender and go quietly.
[Logic 14] 'My signature is a false positive caused by your own leaking grid. Check your calibration.'
Draw your weapon and fight your way out.

title: Vane's Clinical Audit
content: The Iron Watch HQ is a tomb of cold iron and ticking gears. You are strapped into a chair that hums with a low, draining frequency. Overseer Vane paces before you, his brass monocle catching the flickering gaslight. 'You are an impossibility,' he says, his tone dismissive and tired. 'A localized null-point that shouldn't exist. Tell me, traveler, which of my competitors built you? And why are you leaking so much... potential?'

choices.text: 'I'm from Earth. I don't know who built this world.'
[Logic 12] 'I'm a prototype from the Spire Adepts. Release me, or their echoes will find you.'
